other_observers: Doug Faulkner, Alan Bell, Mark Ponsor, Sue Ponsor, inter alia all reported seeing or hearing this bird on 06/27/2009, but not in my presence. Another party was present the second time I heard the bird, but I was unfamiliar with them.; Original observer: Linda Andes-Georges  Many other observers since
specific_location: 1st near the intersection of the Bluebell and Amphitheater Trails just south of the Gregory Canyon parking area in Boulder Mountain Parks.   2nd, about 150 yards to the west up Gregory Canyon itself.
description: A small,two-toned (green and yellow) woodland bird with a bright yellow breast, belly and vent and a greenish-dark gray back with no wing bars  The head and face marking with characteristic the green back darkened into a black cap with black "side-burns", a black eye-line through the eye but a yellow line above the eye and yellow around the eye.Behaviors: Heard singing and then seen in song in the open branches of an elm tree. Later I heard it singing persistently about 200 yards away, but I could not see it at this second hearing as it seemed to be in dense ground cover growth.Call: a loud tu-we, tu-we, tu-we, tu-we given several times per minutePlumage: adult
